reviews.review_msg: He's a great professor—his lectures are very detailed and easy to follow. The workload is also not heavy most of the time. I highly recommend taking his course.
He teaches by lecture rather than textbook and his lectures are superb. He loves you and he wants you to win. Ben Ashby leaves no man, woman, or dog behind. I'd take a bullet for him.

Great lecturer, can explain topics clearly and can help guide you on how to solve problems. Welcomes questions in class and uses Discord for communication, which I found refreshing. Very inclusive of people of any skill level in math. If you have sufficient mathematical maturity, you can excel in this class with less effort than usual.

Very accessible lectures, and incredibly encouraging if you have any questions about the material. Is passionate about biomathematics, and provides lots of optional practice/study problems. I did not know much about biology and pandemics going in, but Dr. Ashby's explanations and connections are memorable, interesting, and easy to understand.
Amazing math prof, and a very interesting course. This course is focused on building mathematical models rather than proofs or computation.
Pretty easy going, chill, and nice! Class was easy to follow but would recommend some review of some fundamental math skills beforehand.

Great prof with easy to follow lectures, doesn't use a textbook so its great to follow his notes which are pretty clear as well. exams and quizzes are very fair, very approachable in class and outside. Had a lot of fun!

The best math teacher I've had in my academic career so far. He cares a lot about teaching the subject and is also very mindful to the students learning experience. Takes feedback from students and implements them and is willing to do anything to help the students! 

His teaching style is very easy to digest, 100% would roccemend Ben as a teacher
willing to take the time to go through any topic with you, one of the best math professors i have had. some of the nicest slides i went through in a math course.
Amazing professor, teaches lecture content well and is engaging. Midterms and quizzes were also very fair.

The goat.
His awesome youtube channel got me through 155 even when I wasnt in his class, huge thanks for putting those up.
Ben is awesome. I love that he publishes his lecture videos. Would definitely recommend taking this class.
In all my time at sfu, Ben Ashby was one of the few profs that was able to take a boring class and make it interesting and easier to learn.
Godly. Take a course with him just for your academic soul's well-being.

Dr. Ashby is clearly passionate about his subject and teaching it to others. If you study the notes, do the homework, and ask for help you will do well in the course. His lectures are clear, the homework is fair, the exams are fair, Ben is reachable outside of class, etc. He sets students up for success.

I did find some of the lectures and notes difficult to follow but Ben was more than happy to clarify afterwards. Homework and exams were fair. Eager to seek student feedback to adjust material e.g. added a review lecture for diffy eqs since most students forgot. So supportive of students. If you're struggling, don't be afraid to ask for help!

I took Math 495 (there was no option but Math 360 is close enough) with Dr. Ben Ashby. He is a great professor, passionate about his subject, and encouraging to students. His homework and exams are very fair, if you study the notes and do the homework you will do well in his class. Always available in office hours and will help you.

Honestly didn't even take a class with him, but my prof would post his lecture videos for MATH 155 and they were so much easier to follow along to compared to my prof's work. His review videos were also very helpful and he's great at explaining concepts. His voice is also easy to listen to lol
very nice asmr type of voice, 10/10 would recommend

My favourite professor so far. Amazing lectures, he records all his lectures and gives lots of examples and practice questions to help us learn. Also made a discord server for students to ask questions in and he will answer every question. Cares about individual learning progress, has sample exams and also loved the numerical aspect of the class :)

Ben cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Had class with him last year and is by far the best prof I've had. This class isn't easy but you won't get a better prof than Ben Ashby.
He makes class enjoyable and considering how uninteresting math155 at 8:30 in the morning can be, he makes it quite enjoyable. 
He has a cool accent too.

Ben is one of the good ones. Take his class if you can because you'll love him. The material can be tough but you won't get a better prof than Ben at sfu. He makes topics interesting and he really cares about the success of his students. Ben if you're reading this, I love you.
as you can tell from everyones review, he is the goat in math, its rlly easy and he's active in the discord. bashby is good at explaining and his lectures are online, so you can skip

Bashby is by far the best math prof I've had. He truly cares about his students successes. He is easy to talk to and provides great lectures. There are pre-lecture videos that need to be watched before class. I totally recommend taking his courses. Oh and I cant forget to include that he may be active on your discord servers for easy communication!

I made this course an elective course so it didn't touch my GPA because I no longer needed it for my Biology degree. Dr. Ashby is an amazing prof though, the course was just very difficult for me, and I think they're unrelated. He takes the time to get you to understand things. Do your online assignments.
